title: Desktop Main Process Analysis â€” Titlebar, Dialogs, IPC, Build System

findings.finding: TitlebarTheme type is minimal â€” only a `mode` field
findings.detail: At packages/desktop/src/preload/types.ts:16-17, TitlebarTheme is `{ mode: "light" | "dark" }`. It is imported by windows.ts as a type-only import.

findings.finding: Darwin (macOS): hidden titleBarStyle with custom traffic light position, no frame:false
findings.detail: In createMainWindow() at windows.ts:149-153 and createLoadingWindow() at windows.ts:197, darwin uses `{ titleBarStyle: "hidden", trafficLightPosition: { x: 12, y: 14 } }`. The frame is NOT set to false â€” Electron's hidden titleBarStyle keeps the native frame but hides the title text, allowing custom traffic light positioning.

findings.finding: Win32: frameless window with custom titleBarOverlay
findings.detail: In createMainWindow() at windows.ts:155-159 and createLoadingWindow() at windows.ts:199-203, win32 uses `{ frame: false, titleBarStyle: "hidden", titleBarOverlay: overlay({ mode }) }`. This creates a fully frameless window with a software-rendered title bar overlay controlled by the overlay() function.

findings.finding: Linux: no titlebar customization at all
findings.detail: Neither createMainWindow() nor createLoadingWindow() have any platform-specific spread for Linux. Linux gets default frame behavior with no titlebar overlay and no hidden titlebar style â€” the platform spread conditions are only for 'darwin' and 'win32'.

findings.finding: overlay() returns a transparent color with symbolColor based on mode
findings.detail: At windows.ts:81-88, overlay() returns `{ color: '#00000000' (fully transparent), symbolColor: mode === 'dark' ? 'white' : 'black', height: max(40, round(40 * zoom)) }`. The color is fully transparent (alpha = 0), meaning the titlebar area itself is invisible â€” the renderer draws its own titlebar behind it. This only applies on Windows.

findings.finding: updateTitlebar() is a no-op on non-Win32
findings.detail: At windows.ts:95-98, updateTitlebar() returns immediately if process.platform !== 'win32'. It calls win.setTitleBarOverlay() with the theme and zoom factor, but only for Win32 windows.

findings.finding: setTitlebar() stores the theme in a WeakMap and calls updateTitlebar()
findings.detail: At windows.ts:90-93, setTitlebar stores the theme in a `titlebarThemes` WeakMap<BrowserWindow, Partial<TitlebarTheme>> (defined at line 57), then calls updateTitlebar(). The WeakMap means the theme is GC'd when the window is destroyed.

findings.finding: updateTitlebar is called from zoom changes as well
findings.detail: updateTitlebar(win) is called from updateZoom() at windows.ts:445-448 and from setZoom() in desktop-menu-actions.ts:80-83. It's also called from the IPC handler for set-zoom-factor at ipc.ts:237.

findings.finding: The set-titlebar IPC channel in ipc.ts passes the full TitlebarTheme from renderer
findings.detail: At ipc.ts:240-245: `ipcMain.handle('set-titlebar', (event, theme: TitlebarTheme) => { ... setTitlebar(win, theme) })`. The renderer can push theme updates to the main process via this IPC channel.

sections.title: 2. ALL dialog.showMessageBox / showSaveDialog / showOpenDialog Calls

findings.finding: [A] wireWindowRecovery â€” showMessageBox with hardcoded English strings
findings.detail: In windows.ts:293-313, the `show()` inner function calls `dialog.showMessageBox(win, { type: 'warning', buttons: ['Relaunch', 'Export Logs', ...], message: 'OpenCode failed to load', detail: '...' })`. All strings are hardcoded: 'Relaunch', 'Export Logs', 'Keep Waiting', 'Quit', 'OpenCode failed to load', 'OpenCode window terminated unexpectedly', 'OpenCode is not responding', 'You can relaunch the app, open the logs, or keep waiting.'

hardcoded_strings.string: You can relaunch the app, open the logs, or keep waiting.

findings.finding: [B] session-export-data IPC handler â€” showSaveDialog
findings.detail: In ipc.ts:110-126: `dialog.showSaveDialog({ title: opts?.title ?? 'Export Session', defaultPath: opts?.defaultPath, filters: opts?.filters })`. Default title is 'Export Session'.

findings.finding: [C] session-import-file IPC handler â€” showOpenDialog
findings.detail: In ipc.ts:132-143: `dialog.showOpenDialog({ title: opts?.title ?? 'Import Session', filters: opts?.filters, properties: ['openFile'] })`. Default title is 'Import Session'.

findings.finding: [D] open-directory-picker IPC handler â€” showOpenDialog
findings.detail: In ipc.ts:182-190: `dialog.showOpenDialog({ properties: ['openDirectory', ...(opts?.multiple ? ['multiSelections'] : []), 'createDirectory'], title: opts?.title ?? 'Choose a folder', defaultPath: opts?.defaultPath })`. Default title is 'Choose a folder'.

findings.finding: [E] open-file-picker IPC handler â€” showOpenDialog
findings.detail: In ipc.ts:200-209: `dialog.showOpenDialog({ properties: ['openFile', ...(opts?.multiple ? ['multiSelections'] : [])], title: opts?.title ?? 'Choose a file', defaultPath: opts?.defaultPath, filters: pickerFilters(opts?.extensions) })`. Default title is 'Choose a file'.

findings.finding: [F] save-file-picker IPC handler â€” showSaveDialog
findings.detail: In ipc.ts:212-217: `dialog.showSaveDialog({ title: opts?.title ?? 'Save file', defaultPath: opts?.defaultPath })`. Default title is 'Save file'.

findings.finding: [G] Updater â€” showMessageBox on check failure
findings.detail: In updater.ts:99-104: `dialog.showMessageBox({ type: 'error', message: 'Update check failed.', title: 'Update Error' })`. All strings hardcoded in English.

findings.finding: [H] Updater â€” showMessageBox on already up-to-date
findings.detail: In updater.ts:109-112: `dialog.showMessageBox({ type: 'info', message: "You're up to date.", title: 'No Updates' })`. All strings hardcoded.

findings.finding: [I] Updater â€” showMessageBox on update ready
findings.detail: In updater.ts:117-125: `dialog.showMessageBox({ type: 'info', message: 'Update ${version} downloaded. Restart now?', title: 'Update Ready', buttons: ['Restart', 'Later'] })`. All strings hardcoded.

findings.finding: No dialog.showMessageBox calls exist in menu.ts
findings.detail: menu.ts (68 lines) does not import `dialog` at all â€” it only uses `Menu`, `MenuItemConstructorOptions`, `BrowserWindow`, and `shell`.

findings.finding: No dialog.showMessageBox calls exist in desktop-menu-actions.ts
findings.detail: desktop-menu-actions.ts (85 lines) does not import `dialog` â€” it only uses `BrowserWindow` and functions from `./windows`.

sections.summary: Total: 9 dialog call sites across 3 files (windows.ts, ipc.ts, updater.ts). All strings are hardcoded English with no i18n/locale mechanism.

sections.title: 3. IPC Mechanism: State Flow Between Renderer and Main

findings.finding: Architecture: contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld + ipcMain.handle/on
findings.detail: The preload script (preload/index.ts) uses `contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld('api', api)` to expose an `ElectronAPI` typed object to the renderer. The renderer accesses `window.api.*`. Communication uses `ipcRenderer.invoke()` (return promise) and `ipcRenderer.send()` (fire-and-forget) on the renderer side, matched by `ipcMain.handle()` (returns value) and `ipcMain.on()` (fire-and-forget) on the main side.

findings.finding: Invoke/Handle channels (request-response, rendererâ†’main)
findings.detail: 33 IPC channels using the invoke/handle pattern (ipcRenderer.invoke â†’ ipcMain.handle):
findings.note: Some github-* channels reference imports from ./github-ipc which does NOT exist as a file on disk. These are likely pending work from an in-progress extraction.

findings.finding: Send/On channels (fire-and-forget, rendererâ†’main)
findings.detail: 3 IPC channels using the send/on pattern (ipcRenderer.send â†’ ipcMain.on):

findings.finding: Send/On channels (mainâ†’renderer, push from main)
findings.detail: 5 IPC channels using webContents.send pattern (main pushes to renderer):
findings.preload_handlers: On the preload side, these are received via `ipcRenderer.on(channel, handler)` and exposed to the renderer as callbacks (e.g., onMenuCommand, onDeepLink, onSqliteMigrationProgress, onPinchZoomEnabledChanged, onZoomFactorChanged). They return an unsubscribe function.

findings.finding: Store system: electron-store with named stores and reserved namespaces
findings.detail: The `getStore()` function (store.ts:12-23) creates/caches electron-store instances keyed by name, with cwd set to `app.getPath('userData')` and dot-notation disabled. All stores use an empty fileExtension (no .json suffix). Generic IPC handlers (store-get, store-set, store-delete, store-clear, store-keys, store-length) expose CRUD to the renderer but block writes to RESERVED_STORE_NAMES: ['desktop-custom-agents', 'desktop-mcp-servers', 'desktop-plugin-config', 'github-auth'] (ipc.ts:26). Those reserved stores have dedicated IPC handlers with valibot validation via `validateAndFilterAgents`/`validateAndFilterMcpServers`/`validateAndFilterPluginConfigs` (imported from `./ipc-validation` which does NOT exist as a file on disk â€” likely pending extraction).

findings.finding: Preferences/Settings flow: no dedicated IPC channel for locale
findings.detail: Looking at the constants (constants.ts:7,8,9,10), the known setting keys are: 'defaultServerUrl', 'wslEnabled', 'pinchZoomEnabled'. There is NO dedicated IPC channel for locale or language preference. The generic store-get/store-set handlers could be used for arbitrary key-value storage by the renderer, but there is no mechanism for the main process to know about locale preferences. The app does not use Electron's `app.getLocale()` or any locale passing from main to renderer at startup.

sections.summary: The IPC system is fully generic: the preload exposes typed methods, ipcMain handles them, and the renderer sees `window.api.*`. There are approximately 50+ channels total. No locale/i18n pass-through exists â€” all dialog strings are hardcoded in English.

findings.finding: Build system: electron-vite for dev/build, electron-builder for packaging
findings.detail: From package.json scripts (lines 13-22): `dev` uses `electron-vite dev`, `build` uses `electron-vite build`, `package` uses `electron-builder --config electron-builder.config.ts`. Typecheck uses `tsgo -b`. Prebuild and predev scripts use `bun`.

findings.finding: Entry point: out/main/index.js (compiled from src/main/index.ts)
findings.detail: The package.json `main` field is `./out/main/index.js`. electron-vite compiles src/main/*.ts to out/main/*.js.

findings.finding: Key dependencies for the main process
findings.detail: Runtime deps: `electron-window-state` (window state persistence), `electron-store` (key-value persistence), `electron-updater` (auto-update), `electron-context-menu`, `electron-log`, `effect` (Effect TS), `@zip.js/zip.js`, `drizzle-orm`, `marked`. Dev deps: `electron` 41.2.1, `electron-builder` ^26, `electron-vite` ^5.

findings.finding: Electron Builder config: three channels (dev, beta, prod)
findings.detail: electron-builder.config.ts exports a config driven by OPENCODE_CHANNEL env var. Dev: appId=ai.opencode.desktop.dev, productName='OpenCode Dev'. Beta: appId=ai.opencode.desktop.beta, publishes to anomalyco/opencode-beta. Prod: appId=ai.opencode.desktop, publishes to anomalyco/opencode. All targets: macOS (dmg+zip), Windows (nsis), Linux (AppImage+deb+rpm).

findings.finding: Platform-specific titlebar: frame:false on Windows only
findings.detail: The Electron window is frameless (frame:false) ONLY on Win32. On macOS it uses hiddenTitleBar with traffic light positioning but keeps the native frame. On Linux it uses the default frame with no customization.

findings.finding: Window state managed by electron-window-state
findings.detail: windows.ts:120-126 uses `windowState({ defaultWidth: 1280, defaultHeight: 800 })` to remember window position/size. The state is managed via `state.manage(win)` at line 170.

observations.observation: Locale/i18n gap: 0 locale plumbing exists between main and renderer
observations.detail: The main process has no way to receive or query the user's chosen locale. The renderer could store a locale preference via the generic store-get/set mechanism, but no main process code reads it. All 9 dialog sites have hardcoded English strings. The menu (menu.ts) uses display strings from `@opencode-ai/app/desktop-menu` which may have its own i18n.

observations.observation: Missing files in working tree: github-ipc.ts and ipc-validation.ts
observations.detail: ipc.ts imports `./github-ipc` (registerGithubIpcHandlers) and `./ipc-validation` (validateAndFilterAgents, validateAndFilterMcpServers, validateAndFilterPluginConfigs). Neither file exists on disk â€” these are referenced in the HEAD version of ipc.ts but the extraction is incomplete. This is a build-breaking state if a full build is attempted.

observations.observation: setTitlebar flows from renderer via IPC
observations.detail: The renderer calls `window.api.setTitlebar({ mode: 'light'|'dark' })` which invokes the 'set-titlebar' IPC handler in ipc.ts. The main process stores the theme in a WeakMap keyed on the window, then calls updateTitlebar() which is a no-op unless platform is win32.

observations.observation: Window recovery dialog blocks on user input and supports round-tripping
observations.detail: wireWindowRecovery's show() loops until the user clicks 'Relaunch' (calls app.relaunch + exit), 'Export Logs' (exports logs and continues waiting if wait=true), or 'Quit'/'Keep Waiting' (exits/resumes). The dialog is modal to the window. Buttons change based on `wait` parameter: 'Keep Waiting' replaces 'Quit'.

observations.observation: All main process state is eagerly loaded via Effect.fnUntraced at startup
observations.detail: index.ts uses Effect.gen() as a main coroutine. The IPC handlers are REGISTERED before app.whenReady() via registerIpcHandlers(deps). The deps object captures callbacks into the main Effect. They are lazily resolved when the renderer invokes them.

findings.finding: github-ipc.ts does not exist on disk but is imported by ipc.ts
findings.detail: ipc.ts:19 imports `./github-ipc` and calls `registerGithubIpcHandlers()` at line 371. The file doesn't exist in src/main/. This appears to be an in-progress extraction where GitHub OAuth code was removed from ipc.ts (visible in unstaged diff: -200 lines) but the extracted file wasn't created. Build would fail for a fresh checkout.

findings.finding: ipc-validation.ts does not exist on disk but is imported by ipc.ts
findings.detail: ipc.ts:22-24 imports `./ipc-validation` for validateAndFilterAgents, validateAndFilterMcpServers, validateAndFilterPluginConfigs. The file doesn't exist in src/main/. Same extraction-in-progress issue.
